point_reviews.reason: The document confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly through the air in droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk. [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00024]
External evidence supports that the virus can land on the face of a nearby person after a sneeze or cough, or be inhaled by others in close proximity or low airflow areas. [node_b:1:6d670fce]
External evidence supports that close contact, such as sitting next to someone or sharing a car, increases the risk of catching COVID-19. [node_b:2:5ef977e0]
The document supports that COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms. [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00004]
The document supports that COVID-19 can spread by touching eyes, nose, or mouth after contact with contaminated surfaces. [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00004]
External evidence supports that COVID-19 can spread by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after contacting contaminated surfaces. [node_c:2:5668c8d6]
The document does not mention risk factors such as living with someone who has COVID-19 and spending time in crowded spaces with poor airflow.

External evidence supports that the virus can spread via larger respiratory droplets and smaller aerosols, and people can be contagious without symptoms. [node_c:1:da9ca351]
